summary.intent: Build a browser-in-browser feature for the Moltis agent platform, allowing users to view and interact with browser sessions (both agent-created and user-created) through the settings UI at /settings/browser.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented live browser viewing with CDP screencast, mouse/keyboard/scroll interaction, URL autocomplete, session history with SQLite persistence, per-agent browser profiles for cookie isolation, cookie persistence across restarts, and stealth anti-detection flags. PR #531 created with ~60 commits.

repo: serde's `#[serde(default)]` only handles missing keys, not explicit `null` values — LLMs send null for all optional fields requiring a `retain(|_, v| !v.is_null())` strip before deserialization
BrowserTool and RealBrowserService each had their own BrowserManager instance; sharing via Arc<OnceCell> was required so agent sessions appear in the UI
CDP `Page.startScreencast` relay exits when `tx.send()` has zero receivers — frames arrive before the subscriber connects, so the relay must drop frames silently instead of exiting
WebSocket broadcast has a per-client subscription filter; `browser.screencast.frame` was missing from the subscription list in websocket.js causing silent frame drops
Chrome's SingletonLock is a symlink to the container hostname; `exists()` follows symlinks and returns false for dangling ones — must use `symlink_metadata()` instead
CDP `mouseWheel` requires both `deltaX` and `deltaY` always present; omitting one when zero causes a CDP error
CDP `Input.dispatchKeyEvent` silently ignores special keys (Backspace, Enter, arrows) without `windowsVirtualKeyCode`
Canvas event listeners attached via `useEffect([])` don't fire when canvas is conditionally rendered — must use ref callback pattern instead
Chrome needs `--restore-session-cookies` flag to persist session cookies (no-expiry) to disk on exit
Same Chrome profile can't be accessed by two containers simultaneously due to SingletonLock; solution is tab-sharing within same container via Arc<Browser>
CDP `Page.frameNavigated` events replace polling for URL changes; `scroll_offset_y` from screencast frame metadata replaces polling for scroll position
BrowserTool's `sandbox_override` field is needed because the tool uses `SandboxRouter.is_sandboxed()` which ignores `[tools.browser] sandbox` config

code.finding: Null-stripping via obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) must happen before serde deserialization of BrowserRequest
Relay task must use lagged(128) receiver and continue on SendError instead of exiting when no subscribers
browser.screencast.frame was missing from subscribeEvents list causing all frames to be dropped by broadcast filter
sandbox_profile_dir() must use symlink_metadata() not exists() to detect dangling SingletonLock symlinks
action_name from Display includes params (e.g. 'mouse_input(x=129, y=231)') so starts_with() not == must be used for action type checks
ActionHook registered in manager() init closure guarantees it's set before first use; registering at startup fails because manager is lazily initialized
canvasRefCallback pattern required instead of useEffect([]) because canvas is conditionally rendered and useEffect fires only once on mount
Tab sharing within same browser instance for same profile_id via Arc<Browser> enables cookie sharing between sessions

workflow: biome format --write uses a different version than the system biome binary; always use the system biome (2.4.6) not npx biome (0.3.3) to match CI
GPG/YubiKey signing requires foreground process — background git commit tasks time out before user can tap
axum 0.8 uses {param} path syntax instead of :param
Chrome stealth flags: --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled + --headless=new + realistic user agent reduces bot detection
CDP mouseWheel always needs both deltaX and deltaY fields, even when zero

friction: YubiKey GPG signing repeatedly blocked background commit tasks requiring multiple retry cycles
biome version mismatch (npx 0.3.3 vs system 2.4.6) caused repeated CI failures that appeared fixed locally
Chrome containers froze completely on LinkedIn due to resource exhaustion — ERR_NETWORK_CHANGED and CDP timeouts with no recovery path
Multiple interacting state machines in the JS (screencasting signal, activeSession, fetching, urlPollTimer) created hard-to-debug race conditions
ActionHook not firing because manager was lazily initialized — registration at startup silently did nothing
Sessions dying from any CDP error because is_connection_error() check was too broad and matched non-fatal timeouts
open_items: WebRTC upgrade (neko architecture) for lower latency and better bandwidth efficiency — noted as right long-term direction
Binary WebSocket frames instead of base64 JSON for ~33% bandwidth reduction
Container memory limits for Docker/Podman users (memory_limit config option)
Containers settings page showing memory/disk usage and up/down status per container
Session cookie persistence e2e tests (planned but only unit tests added; full test requires real browser login)
Multiple browser profiles in single container for memory efficiency (blocked by Chrome's one-profile-per-process limitation)
Google suggestions CORS issue in URL autocomplete (suggestqueries.google.com may not work from all origins)
Video recording of browser sessions (JPEG frames are saved per-action but no video assembly)
Playwright e2e tests for browser interaction (click accuracy, scroll, paste) are stubs that mock the server — need real CDP integration
